name: Morphing Topology
description: A layout that reshapes its zone structure based on AI-detected user context. Zones expand, collapse, merge, and split using animated CSS grid-template changes driven by application state. Integrates with Decantr's composition topology system: zones map to archetype roles (primary, gateway, public, auxiliary), and transitions between zone configurations are smooth and meaningful. For adaptive dashboards, context-aware workspaces, and AI-orchestrated interfaces that reorganize around what the user is doing.

balanced.description: Equal-weight zone distribution. All zones visible at similar sizes in a 2x2 or 3-column grid. Default starting state before context signals arrive. Grid uses fr units for proportional sizing.

focus-expand.description: One zone expanded to 70% of viewport while others collapse to sidebar thumbnails. Triggered when AI detects sustained interaction in a single zone. Expanded zone gets full content rendering; collapsed zones show summary + ZoneLabel only.

merged.description: Two adjacent zones merge into a single larger zone when their content is contextually related. TransitionBorder between them dissolves. The merged zone inherits both zones' content in a combined layout. ContextBadge shows merge reason.

split.description: A single zone divides into two when the AI detects multiple distinct concerns. Animated split with TransitionBorder appearing as a growing divider. Each new zone gets its own ZoneLabel and ContextBadge.

stacked-fallback.description: All zones stacked vertically in a single column. Used when morphing is disabled, on small screens, or when grid animation is not supported. Zones maintain their expand/collapse state as accordion sections.

slots.zone-container: The CSS grid parent that holds all zones. Grid template rows and columns animate between configurations using transition on grid-template-rows and grid-template-columns properties. Container must have explicit dimensions for grid animation to work.
slots.zone-panel: Individual content zone rendered as a MorphZone. Each zone has an ID tied to a Decantr archetype role or section. Content within the zone is independently scrollable. Zone size is driven by grid-area assignment that changes with topology state. Minimum zone size prevents content from becoming unusable.
slots.transition-boundary: The visual and interactive border between adjacent zones. TransitionBorder renders as a subtle divider that can be dragged to manually resize zones. During merge transitions, the border dissolves with opacity animation. During splits, it grows from a center point. Border includes a drag handle on hover.
slots.context-indicator: ContextBadge displaying why the current topology shape was chosen. Shows labels like 'Focused on code review', 'Merged: related data sources', or 'Split: multiple concerns detected'. Badge positioned at the top of the affected zone. Can be dismissed but reappears on next topology change.
slots.layout-controls: LayoutToggle allowing manual override of AI-driven topology. Options: balanced, focus on [zone], merge [zone+zone], reset. Toggle persists user preference and suppresses AI changes until reset. Controls can be placed in a toolbar or as a floating widget.

announcements: Announce topology changes: 'Layout changed: [zone] expanded to focus view'
Announce zone merges: '[zone A] and [zone B] merged'
Announce zone splits: '[zone] split into [zone A] and [zone B]'
keyboard: Tab navigates between zones in DOM order
Ctrl+Arrow keys resize the focused zone's grid area
Ctrl+M merges the focused zone with the adjacent zone
accessibility.reduce_motion: Grid template changes apply instantly without CSS transition. TransitionBorder merge/split animations disabled. Zone resize is immediate. All topology changes still occur but without visual animation.

responsive.mobile: Switch to stacked-fallback preset. Zones render as collapsible accordion panels. Only one zone expanded at a time. LayoutToggle hidden; AI-driven topology changes limited to reordering zones by relevance.
responsive.tablet: Maximum 2-column grid. Focus-expand uses 60/40 split instead of 70/30. TransitionBorder drag handles enlarged for touch (48px hit target). ContextBadge displayed as a dismissible toast rather than inline badge.

visual_brief: CSS Grid container with named zones that animate between configurations. In the balanced preset, zones are evenly distributed in a 2x2 or 3-column grid. Focus-expand preset enlarges one zone to take two-thirds of the space while others shrink. Merged preset combines adjacent zones into a single large panel. Split preset divides a zone into two sub-panels. Zone boundaries show subtle transition borders that animate during layout changes. Each zone has a small label badge in the corner indicating its role (primary, gateway, auxiliary). The stacked-fallback preset arranges all zones in a single column.

composition.ZonePanel: Panel(d-surface, grid-area: dynamic, scrollable) > [ZoneLabel(d-annotation) + ContextBadge?(d-annotation) + Content]
